recipient.name: Sarah Schwiebert
recipient.title: Assistant Director, Advising — Center for Life Beyond Reed (Reed College)

outreach.linkedin_note: Hi Sarah — I just applied to the Assistant Director, Advising role at CLBR. I'm a Portland-local People/program professional (Education Studies background, Master's in Organizational Psychology) drawn to CLBR's developmental model, and would love to connect with someone in the role.

email.subject: Applied to the Assistant Director, Advising role at CLBR — quick hello
email.body:
Hi Sarah,

I recently applied for the Assistant Director, Advising role at the Center for Life Beyond Reed, and I wanted to reach out personally rather than let an application sit in a queue.

What drew me in is how CLBR frames advising — a developmental, holistic model that meets students where they are and uses data to inform the support they get, rather than a transactional placement office. I'm coming to advising from an adjacent direction: I'm a Portland-local People/program professional with a BA in Education Studies and a Master's in Organizational Psychology, and most of my work has been in program design, coaching and upskilling, and turning data into stories that change how people are supported. I haven't been a career or academic advisor, but the developmental orientation behind CLBR's work is exactly where my curiosity and background point me.

I'd genuinely value hearing what the advising team's day-to-day actually looks like — how you balance the one-on-one developmental work with the program side, and what makes someone effective in the role. I'm not looking for a referral, just a peer perspective from someone doing the work. I'll be following my application over the next couple of weeks and would welcome a short exchange whenever it's convenient.

Thank you for reading — and for the work you do with students.

Warmly,
Jamie (Yi-Chieh) Cheng
jamiecheng0103@gmail.com
